section_miscategorizations.text: He reports previously having high triglycerides (265 mg/dL), which improved after starting testosterone replacement on the 28th.
section_miscategorizations.reason: The specific triglyceride value (265 mg/dL) and its improvement with therapy function as objective lab data rather than purely subjective symptom history; however, it is framed as patient-reported history, so the placement in HPI is not seriously misleading.

section_miscategorizations.text: He notes that his peak testosterone levels have been greater than 1500.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Peak testosterone levels are objective lab results and typically belong in Findings, though they are described as patient-reported history here, which does not substantially distort meaning.

section_miscategorizations.text: His last PSA was 0.66 and his last testosterone was greater than 1500.
section_miscategorizations.reason: These are prior lab values that could reasonably be part of the historical course in HPI; however, they are still objective data, so inclusion in Findings is acceptable and not misleading.

section_miscategorizations.text: Cholesterol and triglycerides have been reviewed.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is more of a documentation/management action (review of prior labs) than a specific exam or result; it could fit better under Plan or an A/P-style documentation of data review, but its presence in Findings does not materially mislead.
